args: {"wheel":"compose","worktree":"/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix","branch":"bot/compose-fix","progress_file":"/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md","focus":"Fix compose throughput. The rate limit is NOT the ceiling (proven: 0 429s at 128-way). The ceiling is SERIALIZATION + the judge-cap-of-4. The 0615bc5 await-fix only touched abstractive_writer.py (the pre-pass); the AUTHORITATIVE compose path is still sync-on-event-loop and runs at concurrency 1.0. Apply the seed findings most-impact-first: (1) the CHEAP env wins that are pure launcher/setdefault changes — PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16, PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8, PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1 — verify each is actually read by code before claiming it; (2) the CRITICAL await-wrap: _compose_section_per_basket at multi_section_generator.py:5468 AND :5514 must go through asyncio.to_thread (identical remedy to 0615bc5, one layer up); (3) then the fan-out + one-section-sharding refactors. Do the safe env + await wins FIRST and commit them, THEN attempt the deeper refactors. Never relax the faithfulness gate. Prove each fix with the acceptance observable (achieved concurrency >1, event-loop heartbeat gap small, the knob actually read).","seed_findings":"READ THE FILE /home/polaris/polaris_project/compose_findings.json IN FULL — it is a JSON array of 25 forensic findings (each has: sev, where=file:line, title, mechanism, fix). That array IS your input; dedup it, rank most-severe-first, and turn each into a concrete fix with an acceptance observable. Group by the theme (sync-on-loop await wraps; judge-concurrency env knobs; per-basket/per-sentence fan-out; one-section sharding; dead env vars). Prefer the cheapest highest-impact fixes first."}

final_sota.gap: Compose achieved concurrency is 1 (event loop pinned by the sync compose tail) with the NLI judge hard-capped at 4 and strict_verify serial, while 94% of baskets sit in one tail-scheduled section — versus the 16-24-way off-loop, sharded, judge-parallel pipeline the knobs already claim and 0-429s-at-128-way headroom proves the providers can absorb.

open_p0p1.problem: AUTHORITATIVE compose path _compose_section_per_basket (plain def, verified_compose.py:3262) is called with no await/to_thread from async _run_section — the entire per-basket compose+verify+redraft tail (85-88% of section wall clock) runs ON the event loop, freezing all sibling sections, all writer HTTP callbacks, and every judge time.sleep backoff (entailment_judge.py:1166 15-60s 429 sleeps land on the loop). PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 is fiction; achieved compose concurrency is exactly 1. This is the identical bug class 0615bc5 fixed in abstractive_writer.py, one layer up, still at HEAD. (Dedups findings at :5468, :5514, verified_compose.py:3325 caller-side, and the time.sleep-on-loop finding.)
open_p0p1.fix: At BOTH call sites wrap: `_vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(functools.partial(_compose_section_per_basket, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool, writer_fn=_vc_writer_fn, verify_fn=_vc_verify_fn, edges=getattr(credibility_analysis, 'edges', None), redraft_fn=_vc_redraft_fn, ...))` — identical remedy to 0615bc5. Note _redraft's inner asyncio.run (:4994) stays correct off-loop. Commit this together with the three env wins BEFORE any refactor. Never touch verify_fn semantics (faithfulness gate unchanged).
open_p0p1.acceptance: During phase-2 of a section: (a) event-loop heartbeat probe (asyncio task logging monotonic gaps every 1s) shows max gap < 2s where it previously froze for minutes; (b) log shows two sections' phase-2 progress lines interleaved (achieved section concurrency >1); (c) a 429 backoff sleep no longer stalls sibling sections' writer POSTs.

open_p0p1.problem: Every NLI judge POST is gated by a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore sized by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY — VERIFIED read at judge_concurrency.py:49/81-99, default DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4 at :55, acquired around every POST at entailment_judge.py:155 — and VERIFIED set in NO compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. Once fix #1 lands, all verify threads queue behind 4 slots; the 0615bc5 pre-pass speedup is itself capped at 4. The cap was tuned for a credibility-burst 429 storm, not compose; 0 429s at 128-way proves rate limit is not the ceiling. (Dedups 5 duplicate findings on judge_concurrency.py:55/:178.)
open_p0p1.fix: Add `export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh AND `os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "16")` in the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 setdefault block (launcher/env still wins). Pure config, zero code change. Optional follow-up: phase-aware override via the existing _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE mechanism (judge_concurrency.py:61) so the de-storm stays tight for the credibility burst.
open_p0p1.acceptance: Startup log / one-line probe shows resolve_max_concurrency() == 16 (knob actually read); during compose, >4 judge POSTs observed concurrently in flight (in-flight counter or timestamp-overlap on judge POST start/end logs); 429 rate stays ~0.

open_p0p1.problem: strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop runs SERIAL in production: _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset (VERIFIED read at provenance_generator.py:3390; serial gate `if _verify_workers < 2` at :3664), and the knob is set in no compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. The bounded ThreadPoolExecutor path (:3678-3718, contextvars-copied, cost-reconciled, already gated at 16 in scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py) is dead code in production. strict_verify runs twice per section (:5667, :5804).
open_p0p1.fix: Add `os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY", "8")` to the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 block and `export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh. Zero code change; the parallel path preserves original-order reassembly so kept/dropped verdicts are byte-identical to serial.
open_p0p1.acceptance: Log/probe shows _parallel_verify_workers() == 8 and strict_verify enters the ThreadPoolExecutor branch (knob actually read); strict_verify wall time for a multi-sentence section drops several-fold; kept/dropped verdict set identical to a serial control on the same fixture.

open_p0p1.problem: A hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot for the full PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300s (launcher raised it from the 150s code default without raising the 4-slot pool), and PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES — VERIFIED read via _ENV_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES at entailment_judge.py:199, whose own comment at :196 says the run slate is supposed to set it to 1 — is set NOWHERE, defaulting to 2, so one sentence can burn 3x300s = 900s of slot time. (Dedups both slot-hold findings.)
open_p0p1.fix: Add `export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh + setdefault in run_s5_i3.py (the value the code comment prescribes). Separately consider lowering PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S from 300 back toward 150 (measured p90 13-14s) as a follow-up measured against blank-200 recurrence, since Fix-6 raised it deliberately. Do not weaken fail-closed behavior.
open_p0p1.acceptance: Probe shows resolved total-deadline retries == 1 (knob actually read); a forced-hang test shows a single sentence holds a judge slot for at most 2 x total_s before fail-closed drop, not 3x.

open_p0p1.problem: get_semaphore() rebinds the module-global asyncio.Semaphore whenever the running loop differs (VERIFIED: `if _LLM_SEMAPHORE is None or _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop` rebuilds at full cap), and _redraft creates a fresh ThreadPoolExecutor + `asyncio.run(_run())` per re-draft — a brand-new loop each time — so main-loop/redraft-loop alternation destroys the semaphore ~300x/run, in-flight permits vanish, and PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM bounds nothing (unbounded bursts feed the 429/connection-error storms). Note the judge transport bypasses this semaphore entirely (entailment_judge builds its own httpx client), so the judge cap (fix #2) is the only judge bound. (Dedups 2 findings.)
open_p0p1.fix: In llm_provider.py: key semaphores per-loop in a WeakKeyDictionary[loop -> Semaphore] instead of clobbering one global. In multi_section_generator._redraft: reuse one persistent worker loop (or make _redraft async and await _call_writer on the caller's loop) instead of asyncio.run per call. Keep the blocking fut.result off the event loop (guaranteed once fix #1 lands).
open_p0p1.acceptance: 'Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized' log line appears once per distinct loop (<=2-3 per run), not 300+; instrumented in-flight OpenRouterClient call count never exceeds PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM across a full run with redrafts.

open_p0p1.problem: 94% of baskets (304-326 of 346) land in ONE residual section coroutine that is scheduled LAST behind Semaphore(2) — it starts 50-90 min in and is a single indivisible serial unit, so the run makespan is one serial section and PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS has nothing to parallelize. (Dedups 2 findings; do AFTER the env+await commits.)
open_p0p1.fix: Two independent changes in run_s5_i3.py: (1) shard the residual section's baskets into ceil(N/16)-basket sub-sections composed as separate tasks under sec_sema (basket rendering is independent; reassemble by index, dedup on the concatenated list) and raise PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS to ~4-6 to match; (2) create section tasks sorted by len(section.ev_ids) descending so the biggest work starts at t=0. Also checkpoint at basket/shard granularity so a kill does not discard 95 min of work.
open_p0p1.acceptance: Log shows the residual section's shards STARTing at t~0 and >1 concurrent '[section N] START' with N>=4; total baskets processed across shards == 346 with no dupes/drops in the concatenated output; makespan no longer equals the residual section's solo time.

open_p0p1.problem: Even off-loop (after fix #1), _compose_section_per_basket is a strictly serial basket-after-basket, sentence-after-sentence loop — one blocking ~8.9s judge POST at a time — so intra-section verify concurrency is 1 and the raised judge cap (fix #2) is only exploitable across sections/shards. (Dedups 3 fan-out findings.)
open_p0p1.fix: Fan out over baskets with a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor using the battle-tested contextvars-copy pattern from provenance_generator.py:3678-3718 (_parent_ctx.copy().run), workers ~= PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY; verdicts are order-independent, reassemble by index. Optionally also parallelize the per-sentence verify inside _compose_one_basket the same way. Do NOT change verify_fn/redraft semantics — the faithfulness gate stays byte-identical per sentence.
open_p0p1.acceptance: During one section's phase-2, >1 judge POST concurrently in flight (overlapping POST start/end timestamps); section compose output on a fixture is identical (same kept sentences, same [CITE]/[#calc] tokens) to the serial path; per-run judge telemetry (FX-09 ContextVar) still reconciles.

open_p0p1.problem: async coherence_rewrite_section calls the SYNC verify_sentence_provenance per sentence in a bare for-loop — serial AND on the event loop (default-ON via PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS), freezing all other sections for N_sentences x ~5-9s during every coherence pass.
open_p0p1.fix: Hoist the whole re-verify sweep into one `await asyncio.to_thread(...)`; inside, use a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over only the changed sentences (new != orig). Same acceptance discipline as fix #1; verify semantics untouched.
open_p0p1.acceptance: Event-loop heartbeat gap stays < 2s during the coherence pass; only changed sentences trigger judge calls (log count matches diff count); re-verify verdicts identical to serial control.

open_p0p1.problem: SHA-gated section RESUME never fires (0/5 attempts): a root daemon outside this worktree (/workspace/gear_sync.sh) overwrites the pinned cp3 path ~every 96 min while a 3h+ compose attempt runs, so every relaunch sees a SHA mismatch, declares all checkpoints stale, and recomposes from scratch — a livelock, not a slowdown. The daemon itself is outside the fence; fix must be entirely on our side.
open_p0p1.fix: (a) In the launcher, COPY cp2/cp3/cp4 into the run's own output dir at attempt-1 start and pass the copies (immutable inputs for the attempt's lifetime; 'newest wins' applies between runs, not during one). (b) In run_s5_i3.py, namespace checkpoints per input-SHA (ckpt/<sha12>/section_N_draft.json) so a changed input opens a new namespace instead of discarding a valid one.
open_p0p1.acceptance: Kill a run mid-wave and relaunch while gear_sync keeps mutating the shared cp3: relaunch logs resume-hit > 0 (loads at least the completed section_*_draft.json) and the inputs_sha check passes against the snapshot copies.

summary: Fable-finds-first hamster wheel: Fable diagnoses fast (parallel lenses) -> ranked concrete fix-list -> Opus EXECUTES each fix (no hunting) -> fast verify -> loop until Fable signs off.

phases.title: Diagnose
phases.detail: Fable, parallel lenses: concrete problems at file:line, ranked

phases.title: Fix
phases.detail: Opus applies each ranked fix at file:line, exercises it, commits

phases.title: Verify
phases.detail: run each fix acceptance observable; confirm it took
